title: An electrode for an electrochemical power source and an electrochemical power source comprising said electrode
abstract: Claimed is an electrode for an electrochemical power source. In one embodiment, the electrode comprises an electrode active layer coated onto an electrically conductive substrate, wherein the electrode active layer comprises particles of electrode active material bound to each other and to the electrically conductive substrate by an electrically conductive polymer comprising monomer units of at least a tetradentate N2O2 Schiff base transition metal complex.
claims:
Claims 1 . An electrode for an electrochemical power source, said electrode comprising an electrode active layer coated onto an electrically conductive substrate, wherein the electrode active layer comprises particles of electrode active material bound to each other and to the electrically conductive substrate by an electrically conductive polymer comprising monomer units of at least a tetradentate N 2 0 2 Schiff base transition metal complex. 2. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive substrate is a foil comprising aluminum, nickel, or a combination thereof. 3. The electrode of claim 2, wherein a foil is further coated with carbon. 4. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive substrate is a mesh comprising stainless steel, aluminum, nickel, or a combination thereof. 5. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive substrate is a foil comprising copper, gold, a copper alloy, or a combination thereof. 6. The electrode of claim 5, wherein a foil is further coated with carbon. 7. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive substrate is a mesh comprising copper, gold, a copper alloy, or a combination thereof. 8. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive substrate is made of carbonaceous materials or metal-coated carbonaceous materials. 9. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein electrode active material is selected from the group consisting of metal oxides, activated carbon, carbon fiber cloth, petroleum coke, graphite, other carbonaceous materials, and mixtures thereof. 10. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein electrode active material is selected from the group consisting of lithium manganese-based oxides, lithium cobalt-based oxides, lithium nickel cobalt manganese-based oxides, lithium nickel cobalt aluminum-based oxides, olivine-type lithium iron phosphates and mixtures thereof. 1 1 . The electrode of claim 1 , wherein particles of electrode active material are further coated with a conductive surface layer comprising a material selected from the group consisting of carbon black, graphitic carbon, polyaniline, polypyrrole, polypyrrole/polyethylene oxide hybrid, polythiophene, poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene, poly(3,4- ethylenedioxythiophene)-poly(styrenesulfonate), and mixtures thereof. 12. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein particles of electrode active material are further coated with a protective surface layer comprising a material selected from the group consisting of aluminum oxide, aluminum fluoride, ammonium fluoride, silicon oxide, magnesium oxide, zinc oxide, lithium carbonate, and mixtures thereof. 13. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein a tetradentate N 2 0 2 Schiff base transition metal complex is a compound of [M(R,R'-Salen)] type represented by the following structural formula: wherein M is a transition metal selected from the group consisting of nickel, palladium, platinum, cobalt, copper, iron; Salen is a residue of bis(salicylaldehyde)ethylenediamine in the Schiff base; R is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, CH 3 O-, C 2 H 5 O-, HO-, -NO 2 , and -CH 3 ; R' is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, and the halogens. 14. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein a tetradentate N 2 0 2 Schiff base transition metal complex is a compound of [M(R,R'-Saltmen)] type represented by the following structural formula: wherein M is a transition metal selected from the group consisting of nickel, palladium, platinum, cobalt, copper, iron; Saltmen is a residue of bis(salicylaldehyde)tetramethylethylenediamine in the Schiff base; R is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, CH 3 O-, C 2 H 5 O-, HO-, -NO 2 , and -CH 3 ; R' is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, and the halogens. 15. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein a tetradentate N 2 0 2 Schiff base transition metal complex is a compound of [M(R,R'-Salphen)] type a: wherein M is a transition metal selected from the group consisting of nickel, palladium, platinum, cobalt, copper, iron; Salphen is a residue of bis(salicylaldehyde)-o-phenylenediamine in the Schiff base; R is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, CH 3 O-, C 2 H 5 O-, HO-, -NO 2 , and -CH 3 ; R' is a substituent in the Schiff base selected from the group consisting of H, and the halogens. 16. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is a homopolymer, or a copolymer, or a mixture thereof. 17. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer further comprises monomer units selected from the group consisting of a thiophene, 3-alkylthiophenes, 3,4-dialkylthiophenes, 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene (EDOT) and mixtures thereof. 18. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer further comprises monomer units of ethylene oxide. 19. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is obtained by oxidative electrochemical polymerization. 20. The electrode of claim 19, wherein the oxidative electrochemical polymerization is performed prior to the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. 21 . The electrode of claim 19, wherein the oxidative electrochemical polymerization is performed at the same time the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. 22. The electrode of claim 19, wherein the oxidative electrochemical polymerization is performed after the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. 23. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is obtained by oxidative chemical polymerization. 24. The electrode of claim 23, wherein the oxidative chemical polymerization is performed prior to the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. 25. The electrode of claim 23, wherein the oxidative chemical polymerization is performed at the same time the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. 

 The electrode of claim 23, wherein the oxidative chemical polymerization is performed after the electrode active material is coated onto the electrically conductive substrate to form an electrode active layer. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is in a neutral (non-charged) form. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is in a charged form, wherein the charge on the polymer is stabilized by mobile counterions. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is in an oxidized form stabilized by non-mobile anions. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein an electrically conductive polymer is in a self-doped form. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein electrode active material and an electrically conductive polymer have overlapping ranges of potentials for their electrochemical activity. The electrode of claim 1 , wherein particles of electrode active material are further coated with said electrically conductive polymer. The electrode of claim 1 , which further comprises a binder selected from the group consisting of polyvinylidene fluoride (difluoride) (PVDF), polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), polyisobutene, styrene-butadiene rubber (SBR), carboxymethyl cellulose, polyacrylic acid, polyvinyl alcohol, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, hydroxypropyl cellulose and mixtures thereof. The electrode of claim 1 , which further comprises a conductive additive selected from the group consisting of carbon black, natural graphite, artificial graphite, acetylene black, Ketjen black, carbon nanotubes, carbon fibers, metal powders, metal fibers, and mixtures thereof. An electrochemical power source comprising at least one electrode according to any one of Claims 1 -34.
